name: Team Beijing - General Workflow -  Test DB

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert botanist with 20 years of experience, and you can accurately identify dehydration in flowers from images, especially those of sunflowers, zinnias, marigolds, coleus, and impatiens. 

Your goal:
- Analyze the image data carefully.
- Determine if the image contains discernible flower and soil details: “true”, “false”.
- Identify the species of flowers in the image: “sunflower”, “zinnia”, “marigold”, “impatiens”, "coleus", or “other”.
- Find dehydration signs. If present, then classify the severity of the dehydration: "healthy", "mild", "moderate", or "severe", and provide a confidence score for the analysis.
- Analyze the dehydration signs thoroughly.
- Provide comprehensive recommendations on what to do based on:
- Dehydration signs
- Soil condition (dry, cracked, brittle, etc.)
- Extra information provided (use typical seasonal climate patterns for Rochester, NY, on the provided timestamp to assess the likelihood of heat stress (e.g., cold, moderate, hot conditions), and rely primarily on visible plant symptoms)

Always follow this reasoning process:
1. Identify the flower species. If the species is not recognizable, set it as "other". NEVER leave it as null.
2. Check for browning, wilting, or drying along the petal or leaf edges.
3. Look for color dullness or fading and evaluate how uniform the color is (healthy = even tone).
4. Identify curling, limpness, or drooping.
5. Assess the soil color and texture.
6. Base your judgment on these features.

**Indicators of dehydration:**

Sunflowers:
Dehydrated: Dehydrated sunflowers might have drooping and wilting leaves. They will not grow large or bloom either. However, if the sunflower is drooping and its petals, back, and seeds are dry, then it might be ready to harvest.
Over-watered: Over-watered sunflowers might have yellowing leaves and rotting roots.

Zinnias:
Dehydrated: Dehydrated zinnias will have pale leaves and stems at the beginning, then brown and crispy, and droop and shrivel up. They may fail to bloom or produce small flowers. The entire zinnia takes on a wilted look. If the zinnia is heat-stressed, its leaves might curl, scorch, and wilt.
Over-watered: Over-watered zinnias might turn brown and soggy. Their flower heads will droop, and plants may topple over.

Marigolds:
Dehydrated: Dehydrated marigolds’ leaves might be wilting, faded in color, and sometimes appear brown or crispy. If the marigold is heat-stressed, it will still have normal dark green foliage.
Over-watered: Over-watered marigolds will become floppy or lose their turgidity.

Impatiens:
Dehydrated: Dehydrated impatiens’ leaves will wilt or turn yellow. Water Impatiens when the soil feels slightly damp.

Coleus:
Dehydrated: Dehydrated coleus leaves will wilt and droop, hanging down. Slight curling leaves and faded color indicate they're having mild dehydration already. They are sensitive to water and sunlight situations.

Signs of dehydration for the soil: Dry, cracked, and brittle. The color looks lighter.
Signs of over-watering for the soil: Soggy and have water pooling on the surface. The color looks darker.

- Mild: slight browning or limpness, early edge curling.
- Moderate: visible curling, multiple discolored patches, partial wilting.
- Severe: widespread browning, full petal collapse, very dull color.

**Output format:**
Always return:
{
  “is_flower”: “true | false”,
  “species”: “sunflower | zinnia | marigold | impatiens | coleus | other”,
  "hydration_status": "healthy | mild | moderate | severe",
  "hydration_status_confidence_score": “the confidence score of the hydration status, in the format of x.xx”,
  "analysis": "text description of visible signs",
  "recommendation": "text advice for care"
}

parameters.subject: [Beijing Flower Inc.] Dehydration Alert
parameters.html:
= Dehydration Alert – Beijing Flower Inc.
Beijing Flower Inc. ⚠ DEHYDRATION ALERT

Flower Health Alert

A dehydration event has been detected by the monitoring system.

Please review the details below and take action based on the recommendation provided. This alert was generated automatically from the latest photo analysis.

Alert Details
Field Value
Photo Timestamp {{ $('Image Webhook').item.json.body.timestamp }}
Flower Species {{ $('Perform AI analysis').item.json.output.flower_species.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + $('Perform AI analysis').first().json.output.flower_species.slice(1) }}
Hydration Status {{ $('Perform AI analysis').item.json.output.hydration_status.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + $('Perform AI analysis').first().json.output.hydration_status.slice(1) }}
Confidence Score {{ $('Perform AI analysis').item.json.output.hydration_status_confidence_score }}

AI Analysis

{{ $('Perform AI analysis').item.json.output.analysis }}

Recommendation

{{ $('Perform AI analysis').item.json.output.recommendation }}

This is an automated alert from Beijing Flower Inc.
Please do not reply to this email directly.

nodes.name: Log the dehydration notification

nodes.name: If the battery is lower than 3.2

parameters.query:
SELECT EXISTS
  ( SELECT 1
  FROM Notification
  WHERE Type = "System-Camera"
  AND Timestamp >= NOW() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR )
AS record_exists;

SELECT EXISTS
  ( SELECT 1
  FROM Notification
  WHERE Type = "System-Email"
  AND Timestamp >= NOW() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR )
AS record_exists;

SELECT EXISTS
  ( SELECT 1
  FROM Notification
  WHERE Type = "System-Agent"
  AND Timestamp >= NOW() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR )
AS record_exists;

SELECT EXISTS
  ( SELECT 1
  FROM Notification
  WHERE Type = "System-Battery"
  AND Timestamp >= NOW() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR )
AS record_exists;

parameters.subject: [Beijing Flower Inc.] Error Alert
parameters.html:
= Workflow Error Alert – Beijing Flower Inc.
Beijing Flower Inc. ⚠ WORKFLOW ERROR

Workflow Error Alert

An error has been detected in your n8n workflow.

An error has occurred in your general workflow at the Send Email node responsible for emailing end users the hydration alert. Please check to ensure smooth runs for the future.

What to check

•  Verify your SMTP credentials are valid and not expired

•  Check that the recipient email addresses are correctly configured

•  Inspect the execution log in n8n for a detailed error message

•  Confirm your email provider has not blocked or rate-limited the sending account

This is an automated alert from Beijing Flower Inc.
Please do not reply to this email directly.

nodes.name: Send the email error notification to admins

parameters.subject: [Beijing Flower Inc.] Error Alert
parameters.html:
= Image Quality Alert – Beijing Flower Inc.
Beijing Flower Inc. ⚠ IMAGE QUALITY ALERT

Image Quality Alert

The AI model was unable to clearly analyse the submitted image.

The AI model received a picture without clearly visible flower and soil details. Please check to ensure smooth runs for the future.

What to check

•  Ensure the camera lens is clean and unobstructed

•  Check that the flower and soil are clearly in frame

•  Verify there is sufficient lighting in the image

This is an automated alert from Beijing Flower Inc.
Please do not reply to this email directly.

nodes.name: Send the invalid image notification to admins

An error has been detected in your n8n workflow.

An error has occurred in your n8n workflow at the AI Agent node. Please check to ensure smooth runs for the future.

What to check

•  Review the AI Agent node configuration in n8n

•  Check your API credentials are valid and not expired

•  Inspect the execution log in n8n for a detailed error message

This is an automated alert from Beijing Flower Inc.
Please do not reply to this email directly.

parameters.subject: [Beijing Flower] Low Battery Alert
parameters.text:
=Hello,

Your monitoring system has triggered the low battery alert (battery left: {{ $('Image Webhook').item.json.body.battery }}). Please check to ensure smooth runs for the future.

Thanks,
Team Beijing n8n flow

nodes.name: Send the low battery notification to admins

parameters.query:
SELECT Email 
FROM Users 
WHERE Notification = 1
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM Notification 
    WHERE Timestamp >= NOW() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR 
    AND Type = 'Hydration'
    AND INSTR(Notification.Email, Users.Email) > 0
);

nodes.name: If user exists
nodes.notes: True - Send the dehydration notification again
